summary: Recent changes in the connection transport area of Firefox, particularly for WebRTC (the technology behind video calls), focus on improving stability and performance. By employing a more modern memory allocation method, **the browser now utilizes 'MakeUnique' instead of 'new' for memory management**, which can enhance safety and efficiency. Additionally, updates to transport statistics now allow detailed tracking of security features such as the TLS version and cipher methods used for encrypting data, improving our understanding of the connection's security. These enhancements, all executed on the main thread, aim to streamline browser operations and boost the reliability of real-time communications, making Firefox a more robust platform for online interaction.
